frameworks.name: binocular fusion
frameworks.one_line: single cyclopean percept built early from both eyes' inputs

frameworks.name: interocular suppression
frameworks.one_line: alternative account where one eye's input is suppressed under disparity

core_question: How do the eyes coordinate during reading and is the unified percept of text achieved by fusion or by suppression of one eye's input?
key_assumptions: saccade metrics derive from a unified binocular visual representation
binocular coordination during reading can be probed with dichoptic word presentation
manipulations: dichoptic presentation of target words within normally presented sentence frames
measurement of fixation disparity (crossed vs uncrossed) during reading
key_findings: eyes are often misaligned during reading with ~1 character of fixation disparity on average
both crossed and uncrossed disparities occur and within-fixation vergence reduces but does not eliminate them
despite different retinal inputs, readers perceive a single non-diplopic representation of text
dichoptic target-word experiment indicates early-stage fusion rather than suppression
interpretations: binocular fusion (not suppression) operates at an early stage of reading-related visual processing
saccade targeting in reading is computed from a unified representation combining input from both eyes
